EMPLOYEE HANDBOOK - SECTION 4: LEAVE AND BENEFITS

Last Updated: January 15, 2026
Applies to: All full-time and part-time employees

TABLE OF CONTENTS

4.1  Vacation Leave
4.2  Sick Leave
4.3  Parental Leave
4.4  Bereavement Leave
4.5  Personal Days
4.6  Health Insurance
4.7  Retirement Benefits
4.8  Professional Development
4.9  Flexible Work Arrangements
4.10 Holiday Schedule

---

4.1 VACATION LEAVE

All full-time employees are entitled to paid vacation leave based on their length of service:

| Years of Service | Annual Vacation Days |
|-----------------|---------------------|
| 0 - 2 years     | 15 days             |
| 3 - 5 years     | 20 days             |
| 6 - 10 years    | 25 days             |
| 10+ years       | 30 days             |

Part-time employees accrue vacation on a pro-rata basis.

Vacation days accrue monthly and may be carried over to the following year up to a maximum
of 5 days. Unused days beyond this limit will be forfeited unless approved by management.

Employees must provide at least two weeks' notice before taking vacation leave of five or
more consecutive days. Requests for extended vacation (more than two weeks) must be approved
by the employee's direct supervisor and HR.

---

4.2 SICK LEAVE

Full-time employees receive 10 paid sick days per calendar year. Sick leave does not accrue
and does not carry over to the next year.

Employees may use sick leave for:
- Personal illness or injury
- Medical, dental, or vision appointments
- Caring for an immediate family member who is ill
- Mental health days

Employees absent due to illness for more than three consecutive days must provide a medical
certificate from a licensed healthcare provider.

The company also provides unlimited unpaid sick leave in cases of serious health conditions
covered under the Family and Medical Leave Act (FMLA).

---

4.3 PARENTAL LEAVE

All employees who have been with the company for at least six months are eligible for
parental leave:

Primary Caregiver:
- 12 weeks of paid leave at 100% of base salary
- Available for birth, adoption, or foster care placement
- Must be taken within the first 12 months of the child's arrival

Secondary Caregiver:
- 6 weeks of paid leave at 100% of base salary
- Available under the same conditions

Both parents employed by the company may take leave simultaneously. Leave may be taken
continuously or in blocks of at least one week, subject to manager approval.

Employees on parental leave retain full benefits coverage, and their position (or an
equivalent one) is guaranteed upon return.

---

4.4 BEREAVEMENT LEAVE

Employees are entitled to paid bereavement leave in the event of the death of an immediate
family member:

| Relationship          | Paid Days |
|----------------------|-----------|
| Spouse or partner     | 10 days   |
| Child or parent       | 10 days   |
| Sibling or grandparent| 5 days    |
| In-law                | 3 days    |
| Other relative/friend | Discretionary (up to 2 days) |

Additional unpaid leave may be requested and will be considered on a case-by-case basis.

---

4.5 PERSONAL DAYS

All full-time employees receive 3 personal days per year. These days may be used for any
purpose and do not require a reason to be provided. Personal days:
- Cannot be carried over to the next year
- Cannot be exchanged for cash payment
- Must be requested at least 24 hours in advance (except in emergencies)

---

4.6 HEALTH INSURANCE

The company offers comprehensive health insurance coverage effective from the first day of
employment. Coverage options include:

Plan A - Premium PPO:
- $500 annual deductible
- $25 copay for primary care visits
- $50 copay for specialist visits
- Prescription coverage with tiered copays ($10/$35/$60)
- Dental and vision included
- Employee monthly contribution: $180
- Employee + family monthly contribution: $420

Plan B - High Deductible Health Plan (HDHP):
- $2,000 annual deductible
- 20% coinsurance after deductible
- HSA eligible (company contributes $1,500/year for individuals, $3,000/year for families)
- Dental and vision included
- Employee monthly contribution: $85
- Employee + family monthly contribution: $210

Open enrollment occurs annually in November. New employees have 30 days from their start
date to select a plan.

---

4.7 RETIREMENT BENEFITS

401(k) Plan:
- Company matches 100% of employee contributions up to 4% of base salary
- Vesting schedule: 20% per year of service (fully vested after 5 years)
- Investment options include target-date funds, index funds, and actively managed funds
- Employees may enroll at any time through the benefits portal

Pension Plan (for employees hired before January 1, 2020):
- Defined benefit plan based on years of service and average salary
- Normal retirement age: 65
- Early retirement available at age 55 with reduced benefits

---

4.8 PROFESSIONAL DEVELOPMENT

The company invests in employee growth through:

Annual Learning Stipend:
- $2,000 per year per full-time employee
- Can be used for: conferences, courses, certifications, books, subscriptions
- Unused stipend does not carry over or convert to cash

Time Off for Learning:
- 2 hours per week of dedicated learning time during work hours
- May be accumulated (up to 8 hours) and used for intensive training programs

Mentorship Program:
- All new employees are paired with a senior mentor for their first year
- Mentors receive additional compensation and leadership training

Tuition Reimbursement:
- Up to $5,000 per year for job-related degree programs
- Employee must maintain a B average and remain employed for 2 years after completion

---

4.9 FLEXIBLE WORK ARRANGEMENTS

Remote Work:
- Employees may work remotely up to 3 days per week by default
- Full remote work available for eligible roles with manager and VP approval
- Remote employees must be available during core hours (10 AM - 3 PM in their time zone)

Flexible Hours:
- Core hours: 10 AM - 3 PM
- Flexible start time: 7 AM - 10 AM
- Flexible end time: 3 PM - 6 PM
- Total weekly hours: 40 hours

Compressed Work Week:
- 4-day work week (10 hours per day) available with manager approval
- Must be maintained consistently (not alternating weeks)

---

4.10 HOLIDAY SCHEDULE

The company observes the following paid holidays:

| Holiday                    | Date           |
|---------------------------|----------------|
| New Year's Day            | January 1      |
| Martin Luther King Jr. Day| Third Monday in January |
| Presidents' Day           | Third Monday in February |
| Memorial Day              | Last Monday in May |
| Juneteenth                | June 19        |
| Independence Day          | July 4         |
| Labor Day                 | First Monday in September |
| Thanksgiving              | Fourth Thursday in November |
| Day After Thanksgiving    | Fourth Friday in November |
| Christmas Eve             | December 24    |
| Christmas Day             | December 25    |

If a holiday falls on a weekend, it is observed on the nearest weekday.

Additionally, the company provides two floating holidays per year that may be used for
religious observances, cultural celebrations, or personal preference.
